topical: Benzoyl Peroxide 2.5–5% gel (apply once daily at night)
oral_moderate: Doxycycline 100 mg once daily for 4–6 weeks
oral_severe: Oral Isotretinoin 0.5–1.0 mg/kg/day (requires LFT & triglyceride monitoring)
rosacea_specific: Topical Azelaic Acid 15% gel (apply twice daily)
Acne and Rosacea Photos.monitoring: Monitor liver enzymes and triglycerides during Isotretinoin therapy. Females must confirm negative pregnancy test.
Acne and Rosacea Photos.caution: Isotretinoin is teratogenic. Do not use during pregnancy.

actinic_keratosis: Topical 5-Fluorouracil (5-FU) 5% cream — apply twice daily for 2–4 weeks
Topical Imiquimod 5% cream — apply 3 times/week for 4–16 weeks
Diclofenac 3% gel — apply twice daily for 60–90 days
basal_cell_carcinoma: Mohs Micrographic Surgery (gold standard for high-risk/facial BCC)
Vismodegib 150 mg oral daily (for locally advanced or metastatic BCC)
Actinic Keratosis Basal Cell Carcinoma a.monitoring: Regular skin surveillance every 3–6 months post-treatment. Avoid excessive sun exposure.
Actinic Keratosis Basal Cell Carcinoma a.caution: ⚠️ HIGH-RISK MALIGNANCY — Immediate referral to a dermatologist/oncologist is mandatory.

emollients: Intensive moisturizer/emollient — apply liberally after every bath (cornerstone of therapy)
topical_steroids: Hydrocortisone 1% cream for face/body folds (mild cases)
Mometasone Furoate 0.1% cream for trunk/limbs (moderate cases)
calcineurin_inhibitors: Tacrolimus 0.1% ointment (for sensitive areas: face, neck, folds)
systemic: Dupilumab 300 mg SC every 2 weeks (biologic for moderate–severe refractory cases)
antipruritic: Levocetirizine 5 mg once daily at night
Hydroxyzine 25 mg at night (for severe itch/sleep disturbance)
Atopic Dermatitis Photos.monitoring: Avoid triggers: soaps, detergents, wool, extreme temperature. Identify and eliminate food allergens.
Atopic Dermatitis Photos.caution: Long-term potent steroids can cause skin atrophy. Rotate with calcineurin inhibitors.

first_line: Systemic Prednisolone 0.5–1.5 mg/kg/day (initial control of Pemphigus)
adjuvants: Rituximab 1000 mg IV (2 doses 2 weeks apart — for refractory pemphigus)
bullous_pemphigoid: Superpotent topical steroids (Clobetasol Propionate 0.05%) for localized BP
Doxycycline 100 mg twice daily + Nicotinamide 500 mg TDS (mild BP)
wound_care: Gentle wound dressing with non-adherent dressings
Bullous Disease Photos.monitoring: Monitor for secondary infections (Staphylococcus). Check blood glucose during steroid therapy.
Bullous Disease Photos.caution: ⚠️ Hospitalization may be required for extensive disease. Risk of sepsis.

impetigo_topical: Mupirocin 2% ointment — apply 3 times daily for 5–7 days
Fusidic Acid 2% cream — apply 3 times daily for 5–7 days
cellulitis_oral: Cephalexin 500 mg every 6 hours for 5–7 days
Amoxicillin-Clavulanate 625 mg every 8 hours for 5–7 days
severe_iv: Vancomycin IV (for MRSA-suspected or non-responsive cases)
supportive: Analgesics: Paracetamol 500 mg TDS as needed
Cellulitis Impetigo and other Bacterial.monitoring: Watch for systemic signs: fever, chills, spreading erythema. IV therapy required if no response in 48–72 hrs.
Cellulitis Impetigo and other Bacterial.caution: MRSA should be considered in non-responders or healthcare-associated infections.

topical_steroids: Betamethasone Valerate 0.1% cream for body (short-term use)
antihistamines: Levocetirizine 5 mg at night for itch control
emollients: Apply thick emollient (petroleum jelly or Vaseline Intensive Care) immediately after bathing
Eczema Photos.monitoring: Identify and avoid triggers: soaps, allergens, stress, synthetic fabrics.
Eczema Photos.caution: Avoid long-term use of fluorinated steroids on face. Risk of atrophy, telangiectasia.

mild_to_moderate: Immediately STOP the suspected culprit drug
Oral Prednisolone 0.5–1 mg/kg/day for 5–10 days (for symptomatic relief)
Cetirizine 10 mg or Levocetirizine 5 mg once daily for pruritus
sjs_ten_emergency: Cyclosporine 3–5 mg/kg/day (initiated within 72 hours for best outcome)
Burn unit admission for fluid resuscitation and wound care
supportive: Calamine lotion for topical itch relief
Oral hydration or IV fluids as needed
Exanthems and Drug Eruptions.monitoring: Document all medications taken in the past 4 weeks. ALDEN/SCORTEN score assessment for SJS/TEN.
Exanthems and Drug Eruptions.caution: ⚠️ SJS/TEN mortality rate is 10–30%. This is a LIFE-THREATENING EMERGENCY requiring immediate hospitalization.

androgenetic_alopecia: Topical Minoxidil 5% solution/foam — apply twice daily to scalp
Oral Finasteride 1 mg daily (males only — contraindicated in females of childbearing age)
alopecia_areata: Intralesional Triamcinolone Acetonide 5–10 mg/mL every 4–6 weeks
Baricitinib 2–4 mg daily or Ritlecitinib 50 mg daily (JAK inhibitors for severe AA)
telogen_effluvium: Address underlying cause (iron deficiency, thyroid disorder, nutritional deficiency)
Oral Iron supplements (Ferrous Sulphate 200 mg TDS) if serum ferritin < 40 ng/mL
tinea_capitis: Oral Griseofulvin 10–20 mg/kg/day for 6–8 weeks (first-line in children)
Hair Loss Photos Alopecia and other Hair.monitoring: Check CBC, thyroid function, serum ferritin, Vitamin D levels.
Hair Loss Photos Alopecia and other Hair.caution: Finasteride must not be used by pregnant women or women who may become pregnant.

herpes_simplex: Acyclovir 400 mg TDS for 7–10 days (primary episode)
Valacyclovir 500 mg twice daily for 3–5 days (recurrent episode)
herpes_zoster: Acyclovir 800 mg 5 times daily for 7 days
Valacyclovir 1 g TDS for 7 days (superior bioavailability)
hpv_warts: Imiquimod 5% cream — apply 3 times/week for 16 weeks
Herpes HPV and other STDs Photos.monitoring: Screen for other STDs (HIV, Syphilis, Hepatitis B). Partner notification and treatment required.
Herpes HPV and other STDs Photos.caution: All STD management must include counseling, safe sex education, and partner treatment.

vitiligo: Mometasone Furoate 0.1% cream (once daily for 3 months, then taper)
Oral mini-pulse Betamethasone 5 mg on 2 consecutive days/week (for rapid spreading)
melasma: Triple combination: Hydroquinone 4% + Tretinoin 0.05% + Fluocinolone 0.01% cream — apply at night
Tranexamic Acid 250 mg twice daily (oral, for recalcitrant melasma)
photodermatoses: Strict sun avoidance and broad-spectrum SPF 50+ sunscreen
Oral Hydroxychloroquine 200 mg twice daily for PMLE
Light Diseases and Disorders of Pigmenta.monitoring: Regular SPF 50+ sunscreen use is mandatory. Sun avoidance 10 AM – 4 PM.
Light Diseases and Disorders of Pigmenta.caution: Hydroquinone should not be used continuously for more than 3 months to avoid ochronosis.

cutaneous_lupus: Topical Tacrolimus 0.1% or Clobetasol 0.05% for skin lesions
systemic_lupus: Hydroxychloroquine 400 mg/day (all SLE patients unless contraindicated)
Azathioprine 2–3 mg/kg/day or Mycophenolate Mofetil 2–3 g/day (maintenance)
Belimumab 10 mg/kg IV monthly (biologic for refractory SLE)
supportive: Avoid UV exposure and photosensitizing drugs
Lupus and other Connective Tissue diseas.monitoring: Regular CBC, LFT, RFT, ANA, dsDNA, complement levels (C3, C4) every 3–6 months.
Lupus and other Connective Tissue diseas.caution: ⚠️ SYSTEMIC DISEASE — Requires rheumatologist co-management. Renal involvement must be ruled out.

surgical: Wide Local Excision (WLE) — margins based on Breslow depth (gold standard)
Sentinel Lymph Node Biopsy (SLNB) for lesions > 1 mm Breslow depth
targeted_therapy: Dabrafenib 150 mg twice daily + Trametinib 2 mg once daily (BRAF V600E/V600K mutation)
Vemurafenib 960 mg twice daily (single-agent BRAF inhibitor)
Melanoma Skin Cancer Nevi and Moles.monitoring: Full body skin exam every 3 months for 2 years post-treatment, then 6-monthly thereafter. CT/PET scan for staging.
Melanoma Skin Cancer Nevi and Moles.caution: ⚠️ LIFE-THREATENING MALIGNANCY — Immediate oncology and dermatology referral required. Do NOT delay.

onychomycosis_topical: Ciclopirox 8% nail lacquer — apply daily for 48 weeks
Amorolfine 5% nail lacquer — apply 1–2 times/week for 6–12 months
onychomycosis_oral: Itraconazole 200 mg daily for 3 months OR pulse (400 mg/day for 1 week/month × 3–4 pulses)
nail_psoriasis: Potent topical steroids under occlusion (Clobetasol Propionate 0.05%)
Intralesional Triamcinolone 5 mg/mL into nail matrix
Nail Fungus and other Nail Disease.monitoring: Baseline LFTs before oral antifungal therapy. Recheck at 6 weeks.
Nail Fungus and other Nail Disease.caution: Oral antifungals are hepatotoxic. Avoid in patients with liver disease.

mild: Thorough washing of the affected area with soap and water immediately after contact
moderate_to_severe: Oral Prednisolone 0.5–1 mg/kg/day tapered over 14–21 days
Fexofenadine 180 mg or Cetirizine 10 mg once daily for itch
allergic_contact_dermatitis: Patch testing to identify causative allergen
Poison Ivy Photos and other Contact Derm.monitoring: Identify and permanently avoid the causative agent (nickel, latex, plant allergens).
Poison Ivy Photos and other Contact Derm.caution: Short-course steroids of less than 14 days risk rebound dermatitis.

topical_psoriasis: Calcipotriol 0.005% + Betamethasone Dipropionate 0.064% ointment — once daily
Coal tar preparations 1–10% for scalp psoriasis
systemic_psoriasis: Methotrexate 10–25 mg once weekly (with Folic acid 5 mg supplementation)
biologics_psoriasis: Secukinumab 300 mg SC monthly (after loading dose)
lichen_planus: Topical Clobetasol Propionate 0.05% cream twice daily
Oral Prednisolone 30–40 mg/day (for widespread LP, taper over 4–6 weeks)
Oral Hydroxychloroquine 200 mg twice daily (for refractory LP)
Psoriasis pictures Lichen Planus and rel.monitoring: LFTs and CBC every 3 months during Methotrexate therapy. Chest X-ray baseline.
Psoriasis pictures Lichen Planus and rel.caution: Methotrexate is teratogenic and hepatotoxic. Strict contraception required. Avoid alcohol.

scabies_first_line: Permethrin 5% cream — apply from neck to toe, wash off after 8–14 hours. Repeat in 1 week.
Oral Ivermectin 200 mcg/kg single dose, repeated in 14 days (for crusted/Norwegian scabies)
scabies_adjuncts: Antipruritic: Cetirizine 10 mg or Hydroxyzine 25 mg at night
Crotamiton 10% cream (less effective, alternative for children < 2 years)
lyme_disease: Doxycycline 100 mg twice daily for 14–21 days (early Lyme)
Amoxicillin 500 mg TDS for 14–21 days (children and pregnant women)
Ceftriaxone 2 g IV daily for 14–28 days (Lyme arthritis/neurological)
insect_bites: Calamine lotion or 1% Hydrocortisone cream for local reaction
Oral Cetirizine 10 mg for allergic reaction
Scabies Lyme Disease and other Infestati.monitoring: Treat ALL household contacts simultaneously. Wash all clothing/bedding at 60°C. Lyme: Serology (ELISA + Western blot).
Scabies Lyme Disease and other Infestati.caution: Failure to treat contacts will cause re-infestation. Doxycycline contraindicated in children < 8 years and pregnant women.

seborrheic_keratosis: Cryotherapy with liquid nitrogen (most common approach)
Electrocautery or curettage for thicker lesions
Hydrogen Peroxide 40% topical solution (Eskata — for raised SKs)
dermatofibroma: Reassurance — no treatment required if asymptomatic
Surgical excision if symptomatic or cosmetically concerning
lipoma: Surgical excision under local anesthesia for large/symptomatic lipomas
Seborrheic Keratoses and other Benign Tu.monitoring: Annual skin check to rule out malignant transformation (rare).
Seborrheic Keratoses and other Benign Tu.caution: Seborrheic keratoses are benign — biopsy only if diagnosis is uncertain (to rule out melanoma/BCC).

general_approach: Identify and treat the underlying systemic condition (diabetes, lupus, thyroid, liver disease)
Symptomatic topical treatment based on the skin manifestation type
diabetes_related: Topical Clotrimazole 1% for candidal skin infections
liver_disease: Cholestyramine 4 g TDS for cholestatic pruritus
Ursodeoxycholic Acid for pruritus in primary biliary cirrhosis
Systemic Disease.monitoring: Comprehensive metabolic panel, thyroid function, HbA1c, ANA, CBC as indicated.
Systemic Disease.caution: ⚠️ Skin findings may be the first sign of serious internal disease. Systemic workup is essential.

tinea_topical: Clotrimazole 1% cream — apply twice daily for 4 weeks
Terbinafine 1% cream — apply once daily for 1–2 weeks
Luliconazole 1% cream (once daily for 1–2 weeks — superior for resistant cases)
tinea_oral: Itraconazole 100–200 mg/day for 3–8 weeks (for extensive/refractory dermatophytosis)
Fluconazole 150 mg once weekly for 4 weeks
candidiasis: Topical Clotrimazole 1% or Nystatin cream for skin/mucosal candidiasis
Oral Fluconazole 150 mg single dose (vulvovaginal) or 200 mg loading then 100 mg/day (systemic)
Keep affected skin areas dry; use dusting powder
Tinea Ringworm Candidiasis and other Fun.monitoring: LFTs before and during prolonged oral antifungal therapy. Note drug interactions with statins, warfarin.
Tinea Ringworm Candidiasis and other Fun.caution: India has high rates of antifungal resistance. Avoid fixed-dose combination creams with steroids (Clotrimazole + Betamethasone) as long-term use worsens tinea.

acute_urticaria: Cetirizine 10 mg or Fexofenadine 180 mg once daily
Short course Prednisolone 20–40 mg/day for 3–5 days (for severe acute episode)
chronic_urticaria: Non-sedating H1-antihistamine (Cetirizine/Loratadine) as first-line — up to 4x standard dose
Omalizumab 300 mg SC every 4 weeks (for refractory chronic spontaneous urticaria)
anaphylaxis_emergency: IV Hydrocortisone 200 mg + IV Chlorpheniramine 10 mg
Urticaria Hives.monitoring: Investigate triggers: food, drugs, infections, latex, cold, pressure. Skin prick test if needed.
Urticaria Hives.caution: Anaphylaxis is life-threatening. Prescribe Epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen) for high-risk patients.

infantile_hemangioma: Oral Propranolol 1–3 mg/kg/day (gold standard for problematic infantile hemangiomas)
Topical Timolol 0.5% gel for superficial/small hemangiomas
pyogenic_granuloma: Shave excision followed by electrocautery (most effective)
port_wine_stain: Pulsed Dye Laser (PDL) 585/595 nm (treatment of choice)
cherry_angioma: Electrocautery or Pulsed Dye Laser (cosmetic removal only)
Vascular Tumors.monitoring: Monitor infantile hemangiomas for complications: ulceration, vision obstruction, airway involvement.
Vascular Tumors.caution: Propranolol therapy requires cardiac assessment and blood glucose monitoring in infants.

cutaneous_small_vessel: Rest and leg elevation for leukocytoclastic vasculitis
Colchicine 0.5 mg twice daily (for urticarial vasculitis or recurrent cutaneous vasculitis)
systemic_vasculitis: Prednisolone 1 mg/kg/day + Cyclophosphamide (for ANCA-associated vasculitis: GPA/MPA)
Rituximab 375 mg/m² weekly × 4 doses (alternative to Cyclophosphamide)
supportive: Identify and treat the underlying trigger: infection, drug, autoimmune disease
Vasculitis Photos.monitoring: Urinalysis, CBC, CRP, ESR, ANCA (c-ANCA/p-ANCA), renal function at diagnosis and follow-up.
Vasculitis Photos.caution: ⚠️ Systemic vasculitis with renal/lung involvement is life-threatening. Immediate referral to rheumatologist/nephrologist.

warts: Topical Salicylic Acid 15–26% (apply daily after soaking wart in warm water)
Cryotherapy with liquid nitrogen every 2–3 weeks
Podophyllin 10–25% (applied by clinician for genital warts)
molluscum_contagiosum: Potassium Hydroxide (KOH) 10% solution — apply twice daily until inflammation appears
viral_skin_infections_general: Avoid scratching to prevent autoinoculation and secondary bacterial infection
Warts Molluscum and other Viral Infectio.monitoring: Molluscum is self-limiting in immunocompetent patients (resolves in 6–18 months). Check for immunosuppression if widespread.
Warts Molluscum and other Viral Infectio.caution: Genital warts require STD screening and partner notification. HPV vaccination (Gardasil 9) is recommended.
